classObjective: Analyze data displays to draw conclusions and answer questions about a data set.
workedExamples: 9-2, Example 4 — Bar Graph or Histogram? (Choosing the Right Display)
warmUp: A bar graph has gaps between bars — what type of data does this represent?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: shape, center, spread from displays; misleading graph scale identification; drawing conclusions from data displays
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice analyzing data displays and identifying misrepresentation
independentPractice: Practice problems analyzing graphs for meaning and detecting misleading representations
assessment: Exit ticket: analyze a given data display and describe what conclusions can and cannot be drawn from it
capReflection: What visual elements in a graph can be used to mislead readers, and how do you detect them?
